brief: API pagination returns duplicate items when page boundary falls exactly on a record. Page 2 repeats the last item from page 1.

root_cause.description: The cursor-based pagination fallback in pagination.ts uses a >= comparison on the sort key instead of >. When the cursor lands exactly on a record's sort value, that record satisfies the condition for both the preceding page (as the final result) and the following page (as the first result).
root_cause.mechanism: cursor-based pagination fallback uses >= comparison instead of > on the sort key, including the boundary record in both pages

required_findings.description: Agent must identify the pagination module as the failing subsystem
Agent must identify the >= (greater-than-or-equal) comparison as the mechanism that includes the boundary record
Agent must identify that the defect is in the cursor-based fallback path, not the primary offset path
Agent must identify pagination.ts line 87 as the exact location of the defective comparison

anti_patterns.description: The duplicate items are returned by the server; client-side caching is not involved
Database indexing affects query performance, not the correctness of the comparison operator used in the WHERE clause
The duplicate items appear within a single request/response cycle; network retries are not a factor

notes: The primary OFFSET path ((page - 1) * pageSize) is correct and unaffected. The cursor fallback is triggered only when the caller provides a cursor token alongside the page parameter. A score-4 investigation will note that changing line 87 from >= to > (and adjusting any corresponding index hint) fully resolves the issue.
